name: Nelson Riddle
description: '''Nelson Smock Riddle Jr.''' (June 1, 1921 – October 6, 1985) was an American [[arranger]], [[composer]], bandleader and [[orchestrator]] whose career stretched from the late 1940s to the mid-1980s. He worked with many [[vocalist]]s at [[Capitol Records]], including [[Frank Sinatra]], [[Ella Fitzgerald]], [[Nat King Cole]], [[Judy Garland]], [[Dean Martin]], [[Peggy Lee]], [[Johnny Mathis]], [[Rosemary Clooney]] and [[Keely Smith]]. He scored and arranged music for many films and television shows, earning an [[Academy Award]] and three [[Grammy Awards]]. He found commercial and critical success with a new generation in the 1980s, in a trio of [[platinum albums]] with [[Linda Ronstadt]].
